meta.title: Mizuron Global | Indian Supplier Verification for Asia
meta.description: Independent, buyer-side verification of Indian spice and Ayurvedic ingredient suppliers for food and wellness manufacturers in Japan, Korea, Singapore, and Southeast Asia.
meta.supplierDescription: Mizuron Global helps Indian spice and Ayurvedic ingredient exporters meet international standards for Japan, Korea, Singapore, and Southeast Asia.

hero.eyebrow: India · Singapore · Japan · Korea · SE Asia
hero.clarify: An independent verification partner for Asian buyers — paid only by you, never by the supplier.
hero.sub: Mizuron Global helps food and wellness manufacturers in Japan, Korea, Singapore, and Southeast Asia source from India with complete confidence — independent supplier verification, documentation built for your regulatory market, and no commercial ties to the exporter side.

problems.tag: Why India Sourcing Fails International Buyers
problems.h2: Three structural problems. We were built to close all of them.

items.title: Your sourcing agent is paid by the supplier.
items.detail: Which makes you the product, not the client.
items.text: Most India sourcing agents earn commission from the exporter. This creates a structural conflict that shapes every recommendation they make — and that you have no visibility into. When the supplier and the agent have a commercial relationship, your interests come second.

items.title: Documentation is built for export — not for your market.
items.detail: FSSAI satisfies India. Your customs officer needs more.
items.text: What Japan's MHLW, Korea's MFDS, or Singapore's HSA actually requires at the port is a different document set than what Indian exporters routinely produce. That gap — between 'certified for export' and 'compliant for import' — is where shipments get held.

items.title: The sample was excellent. The third shipment wasn't.
items.text: Without ongoing batch-level oversight, quality drifts. Indian suppliers optimize for the sample when they know they're being evaluated. Curcumin levels, moisture content, residue thresholds — these shift over time unless someone is watching. Continuously.

problems.bridge: None of these problems are about Indian quality. They're about the verification infrastructure — and that's exactly what we've built.

services.h2: Three ways we work with you.
services.h2em: One principle behind all of them.
services.sub: Every engagement is designed to reduce your risk at a specific stage of the India sourcing journey — from evaluating a single supplier to managing an entire import programme. The buyer's interest comes first. Always.

items.title: Verification Report
items.desc: Independent due diligence on a specific Indian ingredient supplier before you place your first order.

items.title: Documentation Retainer
items.desc: Ongoing monthly documentation and compliance management for your active Indian supplier relationships.

items.title: Full Sourcing Project
items.desc: End-to-end: find, verify, and facilitate your first order with a new Indian ingredient supplier.

pilot.title: Pilot engagement — first 3 international clients
pilot.desc: Preferred pilot pricing (~50% of standard rate) in exchange for an anonymised case study and testimonial. Mention "pilot" in your enquiry.

cost.h2: The comparison isn't us vs. free.
cost.h2em: It's us vs. what goes wrong.
cost.sub: International buyers don't compare our fee to nothing. They compare it to a customs hold at Narita, a rejected container in Busan, or six months rebuilding a supply chain from scratch. The math is straightforward once you see it clearly.

risks.label: Customs hold — documentation error at the port of entry
Rejected shipment — quality or specification failure on arrival
Product recall — adulteration or mislabeling at distribution
Time lost on failed supplier trials and re-sourcing

box.h3: One Verification Report. Less than 10% of one failed shipment.
box.quote: "One avoided rejection pays for ten reports. Our clients don't see verification as a cost — they see it as the cheapest insurance in their supply chain."

steps.title: Discovery call
steps.text: We understand your ingredient, specification, regulatory market, and timeline in a 30-minute no-obligation call.

steps.title: Supplier research & shortlisting
steps.text: We identify and screen candidates across India's key sourcing regions against your specification and volume requirements.

steps.title: On-ground verification
steps.text: We verify facilities, certifications, third-party lab results, and documentation — aligned to your specific market's customs requirements.

steps.title: Report & recommendation
steps.text: A professional, structured report with our ranked recommendation and complete supporting documentation.

steps.title: Ongoing monitoring
steps.text: We continue managing documentation and supplier quality on every shipment — so nothing slips through.

ctaBox.sub: Book a 20-minute discovery call. We'll understand your situation and recommend the right engagement — no obligation, no generic pitch.

proof.h2: What a Mizuron Verification Report actually covers
proof.sub: Every report concludes with a clear Proceed, Proceed with Conditions, or Do Not Proceed recommendation — backed by documented evidence across six verification areas.

areas.title: Legal Entity & Facility
areas.desc: GST registration, IEC code, company incorporation, physical facility confirmed against actual address

areas.title: Export History
areas.desc: Verified shipment records through DGFT and customs data — not self-reported claims

areas.title: Certifications Audit
areas.desc: FSSAI, APEDA, Spices Board, organic certificates validated directly with issuing bodies

areas.title: Independent Lab Testing
areas.desc: Third-party lab testing on actual batch — pesticide MRL, heavy metals, microbial, active compounds

areas.title: Supply Chain Traceability
areas.desc: Source region, farm or collection centre documentation, processing facility standards

areas.title: Commercial Verification
areas.desc: Actual MOQ, current price benchmark, lead time verified against real shipping history

proof.reportDesc: Our Verification Reports are 15–25 page professional PDF documents — structured so your compliance officer, procurement team, and customs broker can each find exactly what they need.
proof.comparisonH3: What we verify vs. what a normal sourcing agent does
proof.caseH3: Singapore supplement manufacturer — turmeric extract verification
proof.caseText1: A mid-size nutraceutical company in Singapore needed to verify a new Indian turmeric extract supplier (95% curcumin specification) before committing to a recurring order of 2 MT/year.
proof.caseText2: Independent lab testing revealed curcumin content was consistently 12% below the stated specification across multiple batches — a difference of approximately 10.5g/kg against the claimed 13.0g/kg.
proof.caseSaved: Estimated annual saving: USD 15,000 in over-specification costs

categories.sub: We deliberately stay narrow. Two core categories, deep expertise — because genuine knowledge in a specific domain is worth more than superficial coverage of everything.
categories.spicesDesc: India supplies over 70% of the world's spices. The global market has shifted toward functional spices, culinary authenticity, and clean-label ingredients — all requiring verified documentation that most Indian exporters struggle to provide.

spiceItems.title: Functional Spices
spiceItems.desc: Turmeric (curcumin-verified), black pepper (piperine content confirmed), ginger — for health-positioned food and supplement applications.

spiceItems.title: Culinary Spices
spiceItems.desc: Cumin, coriander, cardamom, chili, fenugreek — verified-origin documentation for food manufacturers needing geographic provenance.

spiceItems.title: Seed Spices
spiceItems.desc: Fennel, ajwain, nigella, mustard seed — sourced from specific Indian growing districts with full traceability.

spiceItems.title: Spice Extracts & Oleoresins
spiceItems.desc: Standardised extracts for nutraceutical and food manufacturers — specification-matched with batch-consistent profiles.

categories.wellnessDesc: Japan and Korea are the world's most sophisticated wellness markets — both actively sourcing Indian Ayurvedic ingredients for supplement manufacturing, nutraceutical formulation, and K-beauty applications.

wellnessItems.title: Adaptogenic Herbs
wellnessItems.desc: Ashwagandha (withanolide-verified), brahmi, shatavari, triphala, tulsi — for supplement manufacturers requiring pharmaceutical-standard documentation.

wellnessItems.title: Beauty & Cosmetic Botanicals
wellnessItems.desc: Neem extract, kumkumadi actives, bakuchi (natural retinol alternative), sandalwood, vetiver — for K-beauty and J-wellness formulation.

wellnessItems.title: Herbal Teas & Functional Blends
wellnessItems.desc: Tulsi, moringa, ginger-lemon, spiced wellness blends — for health retailers and branded beverage manufacturers across Southeast Asia.

wellnessItems.title: Standardised Herbal Extracts
wellnessItems.desc: Botanical extracts for OEM supplement manufacturing — confirmed active constituent profiles with full batch documentation.

categories.ctaBannerSub: If you're sourcing Indian spices or Ayurvedic botanicals not listed here, contact us. Our categories are anchors, not limits.

about.h2: Built to solve a specific problem in a specific corridor.
about.p1: Every industry has a gap that everyone accepts because no one has fixed it yet. In Indian ingredient sourcing, that gap is independent verification.
about.p2: Not certificates — those exist. But the ground-level, buyer-funded confirmation of whether a supplier is who they say they are, whether their quality holds past the third shipment, and whether their documentation actually satisfies what Japan or Korea or Singapore requires at the border. That infrastructure didn't exist in the India-to-Asia corridor. We built it.
about.p3: We focus exclusively on spices and Ayurvedic wellness — two categories — and four target markets: Japan, Korea, Singapore, and Southeast Asia. That's not a limitation. It's a decision. Genuine expertise requires depth, not reach. We'd rather be the best in a narrow lane than mediocre everywhere.
about.quote: "The Indian supply base is genuinely world-class. We've seen suppliers who would be exceptional long-term partners for serious international buyers. The problem was never quality potential — it was the verification infrastructure. We built what should have existed ten years ago."

advantages.title: Gujarat-based. India-present.
advantages.desc: We operate from Ahmedabad — India's largest spice trading hub. On-ground presence means facilities are verified in person, not from a desk in Singapore or Seoul.

advantages.title: 100% buyer-side. Always.
advantages.desc: We take no money from Indian suppliers. No commissions, no referral fees, no bilateral arrangements. Our revenue comes entirely from the international buyer — and that's structural, not aspirational.

advantages.title: Documentation-first methodology.
advantages.desc: Every engagement produces professional documentation your compliance team, customs broker, and procurement officer can act on. Not verbal assurances.

advantages.title: Regulatory depth, not breadth.
advantages.desc: We understand what Japan's MHLW, Korea's MFDS, Singapore's HSA/SFA, and ASEAN frameworks actually require at the port — and we build documentation accordingly.

faq.h2: Questions we hear from procurement teams
faq.sub: If your question isn't answered here, write to us — we respond to every enquiry within one business day.

items.q: What exactly does Mizuron Global verify in a Supplier Verification Report?
items.a: We verify six areas: (1) Legal entity — GST registration, IEC code, company incorporation, physical facility confirmed against actual address; (2) Export history — verified shipment records through DGFT and customs data; (3) Certifications — FSSAI, APEDA, Spices Board, organic certificates validity confirmed directly with issuing bodies; (4) Lab testing — independent third-party testing on the actual batch; (5) Supply chain traceability — source region and processing facility; (6) Commercial reality — actual MOQ, price benchmark, lead time. Every report concludes with a clear Proceed / Proceed with Conditions / Do Not Proceed recommendation.

items.q: How is Mizuron Global different from a standard India sourcing agent?
items.a: The fundamental difference is who pays us. Traditional sourcing agents earn commission from Indian suppliers — which creates a financial incentive to recommend suppliers who pay them best, not suppliers who are best for you. Mizuron Global charges fees directly to buyers and accepts zero payments from suppliers. Additionally, we produce documented verification reports, manage documentation chains on ongoing shipments, and provide regulatory market intelligence.

items.q: Can you verify a supplier we have already found ourselves?
items.a: Yes — this is one of our most common engagement types. Many buyers find potential suppliers through trade shows, IndiaMART, or referrals and want independent verification before committing to a first order. Simply provide us the supplier details and your ingredient specification and we conduct a full Verification Report.

items.q: Do you handle logistics, freight, or shipping?
items.a: No — this is intentional. Mizuron Global is a verification, documentation, and intelligence service. The shipping relationship remains between you and your chosen freight forwarder. We are happy to recommend reliable freight partners for India-to-Japan, India-to-Korea, and India-to-Southeast Asia routes if needed.

items.q: What happens if the Verification Report recommends we do not proceed?
items.a: A Do Not Proceed recommendation means we found material issues — falsified certifications, quality inconsistencies, or documentation gaps that would not survive customs inspection. You receive the full report with complete findings regardless. In most cases we identify 1–2 alternative suppliers in the same category.

items.q: How long does a Verification Report take to deliver?
items.a: Standard delivery is 3–4 weeks from engagement confirmation and payment. This includes facility verification, independent third-party lab testing coordination, certification validation with issuing bodies, and professional report preparation. An expedited 2-week service is available at a 30% surcharge.

Verification Reports: 50% on engagement confirmation, 50% on delivery. Documentation Retainers: monthly in advance. Sourcing Projects: 40% on engagement, 30% on shortlist delivery, 30% on final report. We invoice in USD and accept SWIFT transfer and major payment platforms.

items.q: What is the pilot engagement offer?
items.a: We are accepting three international clients at a preferred pilot rate — approximately 50% of standard Verification Report pricing. In exchange, we ask for permission to use the engagement as an anonymised case study and a written testimonial if you are satisfied.

items.q: Do you offer any satisfaction guarantee?
items.a: For first-time clients, we offer a 50% refund if the Verification Report does not meet the documented standard outlined in our engagement agreement — specifically if any of the six verification areas are incomplete or our recommendation lacks specified supporting evidence.

items.q: Which countries does Mizuron Global currently serve?
items.a: Our current focus markets are Singapore, Japan, South Korea, and Southeast Asia broadly (Thailand, Vietnam, Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines). Singapore is our recommended starting point for buyers new to India sourcing given its English-language business environment and role as a regional procurement hub.

items.q: Can Mizuron Global help with Japan Food Sanitation Act compliance?
items.a: Yes — Japan FSA compliance for Indian-origin spices and botanicals is one of our core specialisations. We understand which pesticide residues commonly fail Japan's positive list system for specific Indian spice categories, and what documentation Japanese customs requires for each ingredient type.

items.q: What Indian certifications do you check and verify?
items.a: For every engagement we verify as applicable: FSSAI license, APEDA registration, Spices Board of India certification, NPOP organic certification, NOP and EU Organic equivalency, ISO 22000 and HACCP, and GI tags where applicable. We confirm validity directly with issuing bodies.

items.q: I am an Indian exporter. Can Mizuron Global help me access Asian markets?
items.a: Yes — we offer structured supplier readiness programs for Indian spice and Ayurvedic ingredient exporters who want to become more credible for Japanese, Korean, and Southeast Asian markets. Engagements range from ₹55,000 for a Readiness Audit to ₹3,20,000 for a full International Standards Alignment.

link.text: Learn more about our Supplier Readiness Programs →

items.q: Will Mizuron Global recommend my company to your international buyers?
items.a: Not through a preferential or commercial arrangement. Suppliers who complete our readiness programs are assessed against the same standards Mizuron uses when verifying on behalf of buyers. Completing a readiness program does not entitle you to buyer introductions or preferential placement.
